name: rapp-go / the Explorer — a quiet Pokemon-Go where you don't catch monsters, you catch the real sky of the real place you walked to.
architecture:
FIVE designs collapse into ONE surface, `rapp-go/`, built as five headless ES modules feeding a single canvas shell — each module owns exactly one seam and the others consume it by contract, never by reaching in.

FILES (new `rapp-go/` dir, static, no build, no CDN):
- `rapp-go/lib/genome.js` — THE ANTI-DRIFT MOVE: one shared module that vendors verbatim from `hologram/index.html` the exact functions all five subsystems need: `momentToGenome` (L1020), `genomeId`+`sha256hex`+`canonical` (L708-720), `geohashEncode/Decode` (L1007/492), `moonPhase`/`tideFromPhase`, `mkRng` (L722), `wmoWord`, `b64enc/b64dec` (L553-554). Header it `// source: hologram/index.html@<sha>` and register with ecosystem-sync/neuron-swarm so a future momentToGenome change is caught. Every other rapp-go file imports FROM here — no scattered copies.
- `rapp-go/lib/weather.js` — the open-meteo fetch + the ENTIRE network-policy story for weather. `fetchSky(lat,lng)` hits the exact URL the cabinet uses (L1087), cache-keyed `wx:<geohash5>:<bucket>` (geohash5≈5km, bucket=floor(now/30min)) in memory+localStorage, in-flight-promise coalescing → at most ONE weather call per ~5km region per 30 min.
- `rapp-go/lib/basket.js` — `keepToBasket(cart)` copied BYTE-FOR-BYTE from L421 (db `rapp-basket` v1, store `eggs`, keyPath `id`, record `{id,egg,title,born,addedAt}`). Byte-identical so cabinet/basket/companion read caught eggs with zero glue and zero DB-version bump.
- `rapp-go/tilemap.js` — the hand-rolled zero-dep canvas slippy map (Web-Mercator XYZ, tile fetch + IndexedDB `rapp-explorer/tiles` cache, drag/pinch/zoom, player halo, marker place+hit-test). Exposes `addMarker/updateMarker/removeMarker`, an `on('tap')` event, `project/unproject`, `metersToPixels`. It is the shared coordinate surface; it owns no POIs, no RNG, no AR.
- `rapp-go/poi.js` — Overpass fetch + tile cache (IndexedDB `rapp-go/tiles`) + the item economy (localStorage `rapp-go.inv/.poi/.lures`). Exposes `refreshPOIs`, `poiStatus`, `spinPOI`, `getInventory`, `spendItem`, `placeLure`, and the `ITEMS`/`DROP_TABLES` data tables.
- `rapp-go/spawn.js` — the `SpawnField`: deterministic gh7-cell × weather-bucket spawn presence/position/genome, plus the photo-capture (`analyzeFrame`) path. Emits a normalized `EncounterContext {cart,id,rarity,source,anchor,weather,moon,capturedFrame?}`.
- `rapp-go/catch.js` — pure catch engine: `eggRarity(cart)` (deterministic from genome content-hash), `throwQuality`, `rollCatch` (crypto RNG, 4-check wobble decomposition, flee-on-break), `mintCaught` → keepToBasket with `caught:{}` provenance stamped OUTSIDE genome. Tuning lives entirely in `ORBS/AIDS/TIERS` tables.
- `rapp-go/ar-view.js` — the AR overlay: getUserMedia rear-cam backdrop + `player.html?bg=world#<b64url>` transparent iframe + DeviceOrientation turn-to-find + flick-to-throw canvas. Owns only the gesture; delegates the roll to catch.js and the mint to basket.js.
- `rapp-go/player.html` — re-vendored `hologram/player.html` + one new `?bg=world` transparent AR mode (wrap the bg fillRect in `if(!TRANSPARENT)`, drop the floor shadow, KEEP the halo).
- `rapp-go/index.html` + `sw.js` + `manifest.webmanifest` — the PWA shell that wires the modules and hosts the canvas.

THE CORE LOOP: live geolocation centers the canvas map → Overpass POIs and deterministic sky-spawns are pinned as markers → walk into a POI's range → spin it for orbs/aids (per-POI cooldown) → walk within 25m of a spawn (or point the camera on a POI with nothing out) → an EncounterContext is built from THAT cell's live sky → AR-view composites the creature into the world → flick-to-throw → catch.js rolls rate = rarity×orb×aid×throw, wobbles, catches-or-breaks-or-flees, consuming an orb → on catch, keepToBasket() writes a byte-identical `hologram-cartridge/1.0` egg into shared `rapp-basket` → three doors: Keep (done), Talk (companion `#<b64url>`), Breed (cabinet `#adopt=`/`#remix=`).

STATE/PERSISTENCE MODEL — three isolated stores so nothing races the basket:
- Shared `rapp-basket` IndexedDB (v1, `eggs`) — READ+WRITE, the one gene pool; caught eggs land here and flow to cabinet/companion/lantern. Never version-bumped by rapp-go.
- `rapp-explorer` + `rapp-go` IndexedDB — map tiles and Overpass POI tiles (own DBs, LRU-evicted by fetchedAt).
- localStorage `rapp-go.*` — inventory, per-POI spin cooldown/count, active lures, `explorer.lastFix`. All wrapped in try/catch so a full/blocked DB degrades to memory+live-network, never crashes.

key_decisions.decision: Map engine: hand-rolled canvas raster-tile map, NOT vendored Leaflet.
key_decisions.choice: Write ~400 lines of Web-Mercator + drawImage in tilemap.js on ONE <canvas>.
key_decisions.why: A following, marker-heavy slippy map uses ~15% of Leaflet's 140KB of DOM machinery, and Leaflet's DOM markers cannot composite with the ecosystem's canvas software-3D aesthetic. One canvas lets spawns, the player halo, and the accuracy ring share the exact pulse/breathe language of the hologram player. Hand-rolled = full control of the tile fetch queue (the only way to honestly honor OSM policy) and auditable/forkable like every other surface. No build step means a DOM lib is just weight.

key_decisions.decision: Tiles: muted near-monochrome basemap (CARTO Positron/dark_matter) with OSM-standard as the guaranteed-policy fallback, in a one-line-swappable PROVIDERS table.
key_decisions.choice: Default to CARTO light_all/dark_all by prefers-color-scheme; OSM standard always wired.
key_decisions.why: A near-monochrome basemap makes the coloured creatures the only saturated thing on screen — dead-on the 'quiet, grounded' brief; the map recedes, the moment is the subject. Provider stays a one-line swap so if volume grows it moves to a self-hostable/keyed source without touching the engine. Both providers send ACAO:* so fetch({mode:'cors'})→createImageBitmap keeps the canvas untainted.

key_decisions.decision: Network exception is bounded to THREE real-place reads, each with a hard cache discipline; everything else stays on-device.
key_decisions.choice: (1) Map tiles: memory-LRU→IndexedDB→network, ≤2 concurrent, drop-off-screen, NO speculative prefetch, cap z, permanent on-canvas attribution. (2) Overpass POIs: query by geohash6 tile-center bbox (coarse — privacy + cache), TTL 14 days, ≥8s throttle, fetch only on tile-miss, endpoint failover (overpass-api.de→kumi mirror), exponential backoff, degrade to cached/empty. (3) open-meteo: ONE fetch per ~5km per 30 min via geohash5+bucket cache + in-flight coalescing.
key_decisions.why: Static hosting with no backend means we ARE the API's guest; caching + low request rate + attribution is the honest, on-taste treatment of real place data and the only way to respect OSM/Overpass/open-meteo policy. Coarse geohash bbox also means the player's precise GPS never leaves the device. Catch RNG, inventory, genome growth, and AR compositing touch zero network.

key_decisions.decision: Spawns are deterministic from PLACE+TIME, but the catch ROLL is honestly random.
key_decisions.choice: Spawn seed = geohash7-cell + '@' + floor(now/30min); presence/position/genome are pure functions of that seed + the cell's live sky. Rarity is a pure function of the genome's content-hash + the real weather it encodes. But every catch roll uses crypto.getRandomValues.
key_decisions.why: This is the soul made technical: two people at the same park in the same half-hour meet the SAME being, a storm literally brings a rarer creature, and you cannot grind or spoof a better one — rarity is faithful to the weather. Yet if the roll were deterministic a creature would be always- or never-catchable; independent crypto rolls keep the throw a real, tense act. Determinism where it means 'this IS that moment', randomness where it means 'will you keep it'.

key_decisions.decision: Content-hash id is sacred: catch/place provenance is stamped OUTSIDE genome.
key_decisions.choice: stampedCart = {...cart, caught:{at,geohash,place,tier,orb,aid,wobbles}}; genome is never touched, id is re-verified equal before minting.
key_decisions.why: Stamping metadata inside genome would change sha256(genome) and break the 'this egg IS that moment' contract and cross-surface dedup. Provenance lives outside genome exactly like home/lineage do today, so a caught weather-creature is byte-identical to a cabinet/companion egg and renders/breeds/talks unchanged.

key_decisions.decision: AR compositing: the EXISTING player.html in a transparent iframe over a getUserMedia backdrop — no real occlusion, no fake floor shadow.
key_decisions.choice: player.html?bg=world (skip bg fillRect, drop shadow, keep halo) as a pointer-events:none iframe; a separate #ar-fx canvas owns the throw gesture and orb physics; DeviceOrientation positions the creature.
key_decisions.why: Reuses the one renderer verbatim (no second orbit loop), works fully offline (cart already grown), and the deliberate absence of fake occlusion + presence of the birth-sky light rig + halo makes the creature read as a soft luminous presence belonging to the scene, not a pasted-on sticker. Camera is display-only, frames never leave the device.

key_decisions.decision: Graceful degradation is a first-class design, not an error path — every unhappy branch stays playable.
key_decisions.choice: No camera / reduced-motion / desktop → 'framed encounter' (creature in its opaque birth-void, identical throw). No orientation → centered creature, drag-to-look. Offline → cached tiles + faint 'off the map' grid + moon-only genome labelled 'the moon's creatures'. No GPS fix → persisted lastFix + 'location is hazy' chip. Sparse rural POIs → 'your own location is a faint well' + lures to self-spawn.
key_decisions.why: The point is the moment, not the spectacle, so the honest fallback must be beautiful, not a punishment. The whole catch loop (cached tiles, already-caught creatures, moon genome, photo path) keeps working with zero network — degrade, never hard-fail.

key_decisions.decision: Battery discipline is engineered, since high-accuracy geolocation + camera + software-3D is the real cost.
key_decisions.choice: watchPosition(highAccuracy) downgrades to a 20s poll when stationary (<10m for ~30s); rAF is gated by dirty|following|animated and idle-sleeps; pause watch+rAF+Overpass on visibilitychange:hidden; DeviceOrientation attached only while a view is on-screen; cap DPR≤2, throttle AR to 30fps; timebox encounters ~90s.
key_decisions.why: Continuous high-accuracy watch + a canvas-3D creature over live video can overheat a phone; these are the concrete knobs that make an all-day walking game survivable without a backend.

phases.title: MVP — walk to a place-born spawn, catch it, it's in your basket
phases.scope: The shared lib layer (genome.js, weather.js, basket.js) vendored verbatim + validated against the hologram source. tilemap.js rendering CARTO/OSM tiles with the IndexedDB tile cache, watchPosition following the player as a breathing halo + accuracy ring, drag-breaks-follow + recenter, on-canvas attribution, and the unhappy paths (permission denied→lastFix, poor accuracy→dimmed ring, offline→cached-tiles+grid). spawn.js computing the deterministic gh7 spawn field from ONE fetchSky call, each spawn a map marker drawn with its OWN live creature thumbnail. Walk within 25m (or tap in v0) → build the immutable EncounterContext → a STUB catch (single hardcoded orb, one RNG roll off rarity) → keepToBasket() → the three deep-link doors (Keep/Talk/Breed). NO Overpass, NO real AR, NO item economy yet.
phases.deliverable: Stand somewhere real, watch the creature of THIS sky appear on a real map, catch it, and open it in the companion to talk to the weather you just caught. 'Gotta catch the weather' working end-to-end into the existing ecosystem.

phases.title: The real catch — throw, wobble, break, flee
phases.scope: Swap the stub for full catch.js: eggRarity from the genome content-hash, the shrinking timing ring + flick aim → throwQuality, rollCatch's 4-check wobble decomposition with crypto RNG, break-free and flee-on-break, birth-palette-tinted wobble/sparkle/flee feedback, haptics+WebAudio (feature-guarded). A hardcoded starter pouch stands in for inventory. catch.html Monte-Carlo self-test asserts known skies land in expected tiers and prints expected orbs-to-catch.
phases.deliverable: A real, tense catch with stakes — a graded throw, a creature that shakes twice and breaks free, a Rare that can flee and take your orb — minting a real egg on success.

phases.title: Points of Power + the item economy
phases.scope: poi.js: Overpass fetch (geohash6 bbox, 14-day cache, ≥8s throttle, mirror failover, degrade) → real named POIs classified into 6 kinds, pinned as spinnable markers on the map. In-range spin → deterministic weighted drops (mkRng(poi.id+':'+spinCount)) → orbs/aids/lures/plates credited to localStorage inventory → per-POI 5-min cooldown with a refill arc. Wire catch.js to spendItem() + read ITEMS[id].catchMult/fleeMult, and the soft bag cap that pressures you to spend by catching. POI-anchored cells get the +1 rarity nudge and always spawn.
phases.deliverable: A full gather↔catch economy over the real map: walk to a fountain for orbs, a monument for the rare prism vessel, spend them to hold a sky, and the place gives again when you return.

phases.title: AR encounters + the photo path
phases.scope: ar-view.js + player.html?bg=world: getUserMedia rear-cam backdrop, transparent player iframe with its halo over the real world, flick-to-throw on #ar-fx driving the same catch.js. Then layer DeviceOrientation turn-to-find + edge chevrons + lock zone, the timing ring in AR, orb-tier chip + berry/aid long-press. The photo/image-capture path: 'Look' button (and default when a POI has nothing out) → getUserMedia still → analyzeFrame (median-cut palette + luma glow) → rapp-snap genome marriage (body from the live sky, skin from the photo) → same encounter→catch→keep. Optional opt-in keepsake postcard (video frame + creature composite).
phases.deliverable: Point your phone at the real world and the sky's creature stands in the air in front of you; turn to find it, flick to catch it; or 'look and keep what you saw' as a photographic diary of a place — the full Pokemon-Go-shaped loop, quiet and grounded.

phases.title: Polish, PWA, and ecosystem hardening
phases.scope: sw.js + manifest for installable offline PWA. Lures/plates fully wired (self-spawn where POIs are sparse, gate the photo path). home:{poi,name} on caught carts so a creature remembers the named place it was caught at. Deep-link handoff polish across cabinet/companion/lantern. Register all vendored lib/* copies with ecosystem-sync/neuron-swarm and add drift self-tests. Tune spawnDensity/cellPrecision/radius and the ORBS/AIDS/TIERS tables against real walks.
phases.deliverable: An installable, offline-capable, drift-tracked surface whose caught skies carry their place's name and flow cleanly through the whole one-gene-pool ecosystem.

mvp_definition: A single `rapp-go/index.html` plus the shared lib (`lib/genome.js`, `lib/weather.js`, `lib/basket.js`) and `tilemap.js` + `spawn.js`. On load it: (1) starts watchPosition and centers the hand-rolled canvas map on CARTO/OSM tiles with the IndexedDB tile cache working (pan offline after first load), following the player as a breathing halo + accuracy ring, with drag-breaks-follow + a recenter button, and handling permission-denied/poor-accuracy/offline gracefully; (2) makes ONE `fetchSky` call and runs `SpawnField.update()` to compute the deterministic geohash7 × 30-min-bucket spawn field for the surrounding cells, drawing each spawn as a map marker rendered with its OWN live creature thumbnail (so you SEE which sky is waiting); (3) when you walk within 25m of a spawn (tap-to-open acceptable in v0), builds the immutable EncounterContext — running `momentToGenome` + `genomeId` on THAT cell's live weather to mint a byte-identical `hologram-cartridge/1.0` egg with `born.coord = geohash9·epochMs`, `born.from = 'live T°C · code W · wind · day/night'`; (4) a STUB catch — one hardcoded orb, a single RNG roll off the creature's rarity — then `keepToBasket()` writes it into the shared `rapp-basket` store; (5) three existing doors: Keep (done), Talk → `companion#<b64url>`, Breed → `cabinet#adopt=<b64url>`. That is the shortest path to 'walk somewhere → a place-born encounter appears → catch it → it lands in your basket and you can talk to the weather you caught' — with the real catch engine, POI economy, and AR each layering in behind the same `addMarker`/`EncounterContext`/`keepToBasket` seams without touching this core.
soul_statement: You do not catch generic monsters — you catch the real sky of a real place you physically walked to, this exact half-hour. The creature is deterministically grown from that spot's live weather, moon, and time, so a storm honestly brings a rarer being, two people at the same park meet the same one, and no amount of grinding or spoofing buys a better creature — rarity is faithful to the world. Everything about the presentation defends that: a muted near-monochrome map so the only saturated, moving thing is the creature born of your sky; a player that is a soft breathing halo, not an arcade icon; stops that are the place's own named fountain and war memorial, not neon towers; items that are matter — glass, dew, prism, salt, honey — not Ultra Balls; a catch whose only 'stat' is the wobble count you feel; an AR creature that simply stands luminous in the actual air with no fake shadow; and an honest camera-free 'framed' fallback treated as beautiful, not a punishment. Persistent OpenStreetMap attribution and a strict low-rate cache are a quiet act of respect for the real map under your feet. The whole economy exists only to make one moment worth keeping — you gather a little, spend it to hold a single real sky, and it becomes a keepsake you can then talk to and breed. Loud games catch monsters; this one presses a flower.
open_questions: Keepsake coordinate precision: born.coord bakes geohash-9 (~5m) into an egg people may share. Keep it at gh9 for consistency with the cabinet's momentPhrase/lineage captions, or expose a coarser precision knob for privacy at the cost of slightly coarser captions? The whole ecosystem currently parses gh9.

CARTO's free basemap tier requires OSM+CARTO credit and has soft volume limits — acceptable to lead with it (muted look is on-taste) and keep OSM-standard as the always-safe fallback, or default straight to OSM-standard to avoid any third-party tile dependency at all?

Anti-cheese scope: with no backend a player can refresh to re-roll an encounter or spoof GPS. Accept this as a single-player keepsake game (the only person cheated is the player), or bind encounters harder to replenish-gated inventory so cheesing still costs real orbs?

Spawn tuning against a real walk: defaults are gh7 cells / 0.30 density / 300m radius / 12 max-visible / 30-min buckets — these need one real walking session to feel neither arcade-spammy nor dead; is there a target walk/city to tune against?

Photo-path genome default: the rapp-snap marriage (body from live sky, skin from photo) keeps every catch tied to THIS place, but a pure-photo genome is available as a toggle — which is the default for the image-capture encounter?
Should lures be place-social (a lured POI advertises a shared spawn descriptor) even though there is no backend, or purely a local self-spawn convenience for sparse areas?
